column1: Fig. 4. CCPN interfered bacterial membranes and inhibited S. aureus biofilm formation. A:TEM image of untreated S. aureus and treated by CCPN (× 20 000).
B: Fluorescence intensity of PI in S. aureus was detected after CCPN treatment. C: SEM images (×100 and × 20 000) of S. aureus ATCC29213 treated with CCPN. D:
Effect of CCPN on the growth rate of bacterial biofilm. E: Effect of CCPN on the number of viable bacteria in bacterial biofilm. F: Elimination rate of bacterial biofilm
by CCPN. G: AFM images of S. aureus ATCC29213 treated with CCPN. H: Effect of CCPN on biofilm height. I: Effect of CCPN on the roughness of biofilm. The data are
expressed as the mean ± SEM (n = 3). P values were determined by one-way ANOVA. *P < 0.05, **P < 0.01, ****P < 0.0001 vs the control group (0 μg/mL CCPN).
#P < 0.05, ##P < 0.01, ####  P < 0.0001 vs the MIC group (156.3 μg/mL CCPN).
